entries.what_they_read: Big headline says 'Trade player tokens that move with real stats' and there's a $100 claim button. Scrolled down and read the 'New here? 10-second version' block pretty carefully.
entries.their_interpretation: It's a fake stock market for NBA players using play money — no real cash involved, supposedly. The $100 is pretend, which is either genuinely harmless or the hook before they ask for something real.
entries.their_decision: I'm hovering over 'Claim $100 →' but I haven't clicked it. I'm reading the Terms section first.
entries.their_rationale: Every rugpull I've seen started with free money and no signup required — that's not a feature, that's a flag. The 'no account, no password' thing sounds frictionless on purpose, and frictionless on purpose usually means they're collecting something else.
confusion_notes: 'No seed phrase' — why mention seed phrases at all if this is just fake money? That implies crypto is somewhere in the stack and they're trying to pre-empt my fear of it.
'The gap is your edge' — edge over what? Over other fake-money traders? The stakes framing feels borrowed from real trading and doesn't quite land in a demo context.
'Devnet' — okay so this IS crypto infrastructure. Devnet is a blockchain test environment. So there IS a wallet somewhere, even if it's not real money. That's not explained.
'AWAITING TIP-OFF' — is this a live game thing? Does the app only work during NBA games? What happens if I sign up now?
Who built this and why? 'Built by FanShare' tells me nothing. No team page, no Twitter link, no faces.
first_impression_elements: Big 'Claim $100 →' button — orange or prominent enough that I clocked it immediately and then immediately got suspicious
'No account yet · $100 · no real money · no seed phrase' subline — this is doing a lot of reassurance work fast, which made me more suspicious not less
'Devnet' label in the top bar — tiny but I caught it, and it told me more about the tech stack than the whole landing page did
ignored_elements: The LeBron $5.59 vs $4.20 example — I skimmed it, didn't process it, felt like filler
The 'Buying raises the price for the next person' mechanics paragraph — I'll care about this if I ever actually trade, which is not tonight
💬Feedback button — completely invisible to me psychologically

first_impression_elements: Giant orange 'real stats.' headline — loud, designed to grab
Yellow 'Claim $100' CTA button — screams free money, which is exactly what a rugpull screams
Dark grid background — someone watched too many crypto startup videos
ignored_elements: DEVNET label in the nav — buried, easy to miss, but actually the most important thing on the page
data.visual_notes: Clean dark aesthetic with decent typographic hierarchy — this isn't thrown together. But 'DEVNET' and 'PRACTICE MODE' plastered everywhere while a button says 'Claim $100' is a contradiction that my nervous system clocks immediately. Zero social proof, no team, no about, no footer — just a CTA and vibes. I've seen this layout before and I didn't get my money back.
